title: ericameria parryi var. attenuata
intro: Ericameria parryi var. attenuata, also known as Parry's goldenrod, is a shrubby perennial plant in the sunflower family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to have medicinal properties such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and preventing infection. The health benefits of Parry's goldenrod include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues, as well as its ability to support immune function and protect against oxidative stress. Parry's goldenrod can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
